subject: Ecology
title: Reef Biodiversity
description: A fast breakdown of Reef Biodiversity for builders.

hook.text: A single coral reef can host more species than an entire European country.

buildup.text: Fish, sponges, eels, shrimp, sea slugs, and octopuses all share the same reef structure.

discovery.text: Reefs create thousands of micro-habitats: crevices, caves, sand patches, and ledges.

twist.text: Many reef species are active only at night—a completely different community takes over.

climax.text: This density of life makes reefs the most biodiverse marine ecosystems on the planet.

punchline.text: Reefs pack more life per square meter than anywhere.

quiz.question: Why are coral reefs so biodiverse?
options: They are the deepest ocean zones
